easy.title: Build a CSS Module Button Component
easy.description: Create a reusable button component using CSS Modules. Define a Button.module.css file with base button styles, then compose variant classes (primary, secondary, danger) that build on the base. Use the composes keyword for shared styles. Import and use the styles in a React component.

medium.title: Build a Card Layout System with Cross-File Composition
medium.description: Create a Card component system using CSS Modules with composition across multiple module files. Build a shared.module.css with typography and spacing utility classes, and a Card.module.css that composes from those shared utilities. Include card header, body, and footer sections with a featured variant.
medium.starterCode:
/* shared.module.css — define reusable .heading, .bodyText, .spacing classes */
/* Card.module.css — compose from shared and add card-specific styles */

hard.title: Build a Themed Dashboard with Global Styles and CSS Modules
hard.description: Build a multi-component dashboard layout using CSS Modules that combines locally scoped component styles with global theme variables. Include a Sidebar component, a Header component, and a MainContent component — each with their own .module.css file. Use :global() for the layout grid and CSS custom properties for theme tokens shared across modules.
